records.display_name: 34th Street Enhanced Bus Priority, Manhattan

description: Exploring bus and pedestrian safety enhancements on 34th Street, located within the Congestion Pricing tolling zone. Identified as an important corridor for facilitating safe and easy travel within the Central Business District. Aims to improve bus speeds and reliability, and safety.
Enhanced bus priority project on 34th Street with curbside bus lane, real-time arrival information, left-turn signal priority, and taxi video enforcement

Project studying 34th Street from 12th Avenue to FDR Drive for enhanced bus priority treatments including bus lane improvements, pedestrian safety, and curb management. Identified in NYC DOT's Connecting to the Core Report.
Enhanced bus priority project on 34th Street from 12th Avenue to FDR Drive

Draft proposal for a 14th Street-style busway between 3rd Av and 9th Av on 34th Street, with existing bus lanes maintained on the rest of the corridor, new pedestrian safety improvements, and curb management for loading/deliveries
Draft proposal for a 14th Street style busway between 3rd Ave and 9th Ave on 34th Street in Manhattan
Study for enhanced bus priority on 34th Street, presented to Community Board 6 in February 2025. Study area: 12th Avenue to FDR Drive. Identified in NYC DOT's Connecting to the Core Report.
Proposed 14th Street style busway on 34th Street between 3rd Ave and 9th Ave, together with pedestrian safety improvements and curb management for loading/deliveries

payload.description: Exploring bus and pedestrian safety enhancements on 34th Street, located within the Congestion Pricing tolling zone. Identified as an important corridor for facilitating safe and easy travel within the Central Business District. Aims to improve bus speeds and reliability, and safety.
payload.location: 34th Street from 12th Avenue to FDR Drive

location_normalized.raw_text: 34th Street from 12th Avenue to FDR Drive

records.display_name: First Avenue/Second Avenue SBS

description: 8.5 miles from South Ferry to 125th Street. Currently served by M15 bus route. SBS corridor on First and Second Avenues in Manhattan.
SBS project along First and Second Avenues on the East Side of Manhattan, from South Ferry to 125th Street
Proposed SBS project on First and Second Avenues from South Ferry to 125th Street, replacing existing M15 Limited service
SBS along First Avenue and Second Avenue from South Ferry to 125th Street, launching October 10, 2010
M15 Select Bus Service along First Avenue and Second Avenue, adding over 10 miles of new and improved bus facilities and doubling protected bike lanes in New York City
Select Bus Service corridor on First and Second Avenues from 125th Street to South Ferry, currently served by M15 bus route
SBS service on First Avenue and Second Avenue from South Ferry to 125th Street, replacing M15 local bus route. Service begins October 2010.

Phase I BRT corridor identified by NYC BRT Study in 2006; 8.5 miles from South Ferry to 125th Street; includes bus lanes, transit signal priority, off-board fare collection, and protected bicycle paths
Phase I BRT corridor identified by NYC BRT Study in 2006, running 8.5 miles from South Ferry to 125th Street, currently served by M15 bus route

Implementation of Select Bus Service on First and Second Avenues from Houston Street to 125th Street, including bus lanes, off-board fare collection, low-floor three-door buses, transit signal priority, and bike lanes
Select Bus Service (SBS) on First and Second Avenues in Manhattan, launched M15 SBS October 10, 2010, with dedicated bus lanes, off-board fare payment, and revised station spacing.

Select Bus Service on First and Second Avenues in Manhattan, launched October 10, 2010 with dedicated bus lanes, NYPD and camera enforcement, off-board fare payment, revised station spacing, and low-floor three-door buses
Phase I BRT route planned to begin service in 2010 to help relieve subway crowding on Lexington Avenue lines
Phase I BRT route along First and Second Avenues in Manhattan; will help relieve subway crowding on Lexington Avenue lines
project_name: First Avenue/Second Avenue Select Bus Service

payload.description: 8.5 miles from South Ferry to 125th Street. Currently served by M15 bus route. SBS corridor on First and Second Avenues in Manhattan.

records.display_name: BX12-SBS - ABLE route

description: First BRT implementation on Bx12 route with 20% travel time savings
Fordham Road Select Bus Service
Existing SBS route toured by the CAC as a reference example.
Fordham Road Select Bus Service, cited as example of SBS features and results
SBS route on Fordham Road and Pelham Parkway. Used as an example of SBS experience in NYC.
Bx12 Select Bus Service on Fordham Road. Referenced as an example of SBS results.
New York City's first SBS route, on Fordham Road-Pelham Parkway
Bx12 Select Bus Service on Fordham Road, cited as example of SBS results.
Existing SBS route used as example of SBS features and results
Bx12 Select Bus Service shown as an SBS corridor on the Webster Avenue map.
Existing SBS route shown on map: Fordham Rd/Pelham Pkwy
Bx12 SBS provides a key crosstown transit connection in the Bronx. Implemented June 2008 on Fordham Road/Pelham Parkway.
NYC's first BRT route, on Fordham Road in the Bronx, launched summer 2008
Select Bus Service on Fordham Road corridor. 53,100 use the Bx12 SBS and Local.
First SBS route in the Bronx, launched in 2008; busiest bus route in the Bronx and second busiest in NYC
First Select Bus Service route launched in 2008 on Fordham Road corridor
First SBS route launched in 2008. Busiest bus route in the Bronx, second busiest in NYC after the M15. 37,000 daily ridership.
First SBS route launched in 2008. Busiest bus route in the Bronx, second busiest in NYC after the M15.
First SBS route in the Bronx, second busiest bus route in NYC after the M15
Route serving Fordham Road corridor
First SBS route launched in 2008 on Fordham Road; second highest ridership route in NYC after M15
First SBS route on Fordham Road, launched in 2008. Busiest bus route in the Bronx.
First SBS route launched in 2008 on Fordham Road corridor
Select Bus Service route referenced as the route where William O'Leary was originally assigned to enforce fare payment.
note: ABLE cameras operated on this route through 2023
Displayed in MTA App screenshots as Bx12+ and Bx12
